match.def: to shift; to turn off to one side
an omen or prediction of coming evil
very eager for food, satisfaction, or gratification
a person held by another to secure demands of money or control
unable to be conquered, overcame, or subdued
to be acting spontaneously or in excitement
a projectile of guns or explosive military items
a learning disability involving difficulties in processing written language

gap.sentence: The train was shunted onto a side track to let the express pass.
gap.ja: 急行列車を通すために、その電車は支線へと転換された。

gap.sentence: After hiking for six hours, the group was ravenous and devoured everything on the table.
gap.ja: 6時間ハイキングした後、グループは腹ぺこで、テーブルの上のものを何でも食べ尽くした。

gap.sentence: The championship team felt invincible after winning fifteen games in a row.
gap.ja: 15連勝したチャンピオンチームは、自分たちが無敵だと感じていた。

gap.sentence: After years of poor management, the company's collapse was inevitable.
gap.ja: 何年もの経営不振の末、その会社の倒産は避けられないことだった。

gap.sentence: The surgeon meticulously cleaned and stitched the wound to minimize scarring.
gap.ja: 外科医は傷跡を最小限にするために、傷口を念入りに洗浄し縫合した。

opts: an omen or prediction of coming evil
a person held to secure demands of money or control
to be acting spontaneously or in excitement
a proposed outline of a trip
a projectile of guns or explosive military items

dict.text: She thought she could have walked the paths blindfolded, navigating by her green-sense alone.
There was a sudden silence all around, broken only by the dull cracking of the flames.
He used to go off for hours on his own, daydreaming and sketching.
